                                                                               Paul M. Schwab*

                     The difficulties in obtaining employment that        Many young people will have found jobs dur-
                  confront teenagers in our large cities become        ing the winter and spring and, therefore, will not
                  particularly acute during the summer months          be hunting for jobs initially during the summer
                  (June-August), when large numbers of these           months. Roughly 1.7 million teenagers were
                  young people enter the job market at the same        estimated to be employed in these 20 metropol-
                  time. In light of the recent national trend toward   itan areas in the spring of 1970, slightly more
                  higher unemployment, the job-hunting obstacles       than were employed a year ago. Many of these

                                                               Technical Note

                                                    Additional information concerning the preparation
                                                    of the labor force, employment, hours and earnings,
                                                    and labor turnover series—concepts and scope,
                                                    survey methods, and limitations—is contained in
                                                    technical notes for each of these series, available
                                                    from the Bureau of Labor Statistics free of charge.


                 INTRODUCTION                                                    Relation between the household and payroll series

                 three major sources: (1) household interviews, (2) pay-         another, each providing significant types of information
                 roll reports from employers, and (3) administrative             that the other cannot suitably supply. Population charac-

                                                                    ATTENTION
                       As discussed in the Technical Note, the Bureau periodically adjusts the industry employment series to a
                 recent benchmark to improve their accuracy. These adjustments may also affect the hours and earnings series
                 because employment levels are used as weights. Industry data for all national series shown in this report have
                 been adjusted to March 1968 benchmarks. Data from April 1968 forward are subject to revision at the time of
                 the next benchmark.

                       Beginning with the July 1969 and subsequent issues of Employment and Earnings, the national data in sec-
                 tions B, C, and D supersede those published in previous issues, as well as those appearing in the Handbook of
                 Labor Statistics, 1969. Historical data for a particular industry, comparable to those currently appearing in
                 Employment and Earnings, are available upon request to any of the BLS regional offices (see inside back cover
                 for addresses) or to the Bureau of Labor Statistics, Washington, D.C, 20212.
